requestBody.description: Delete a list of Assets by their ID's

post.description: Copy an Asset to a new location

id.description: Identifier of the asset to be copied

post.description: Create a new asset.

200.description:
Asset create success response. 

When `is_realtime_asset` was set to `true` in the request, a simplified payload will be returned.

requestBody.description:
While supported, we encourage you to switch from `application/x-ww-form-urlencoded` to `application/json` in order to support future workflows that will only be supported through JSON.

When `is_realtime_upload` is `true`, call must be made in `application/json`.

name.description:
The name the asset should have in Frame.io. This value does not have to match the name of the file on disk; it can be whatever you want it to be in Frame.io.

Required if `is_realtime_upload` is `false`.

If `is_realtime_upload` is `true`, must containa valid extension in the form of `".[ext]"`, unless `extension` is provided, in which case this field may be `null` or omitted.

extension.description:
Required when `is_realtime_upload` is `true`, when `name` is not known at time of asset creation. If `name` is provided, must match extension of that value.

Ignored when `is_realtime_upload` is `false`.

When supplied, asset will be creted with `name` value of `"[new file].extension"`.

filetype.description:
MIME type like `video/quicktime`. Many languages have built in utilities for detecting file mimetype (see Go and Python for examples).

Required if `is_realtime_upload` is `true`.

is_realtime_upload.description:
If `true`, File data for this asset is to be uploaded through the Real-time Uploads API.

If `false`, File data for this asset will be uploaded through the default API.

Must be `false` if `filetype` is not `file`.

filesize.description:
File size in bytes.

Required if `is_realtime_upload` is not `true`.

get.description: Get child elements of a given asset. Can be used to get the items in a review link, version stack, folder, as well as including an asset to use on the cover of a folder.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The sorting order for the returned list of Assets. Supporting chaining like `comment_count,id`. Also supports reversing the order with `-` i.e. `-comment_count,id` or `filetype,-id`.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The current page number in this Paginated Response

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of pages in this Paginated Response

post.summary: Request upload URLs for real-time upload
post.description: Register real-time upload parts and generate presigned AWS S3 upload PUT URLs.

200.description: Returns array of upload URLs for created parts.

requestBody.description: Enumerates parts that upload URLs should be created for.

parts.description: List of file upload parts to register with Frame.

asset_name.description:
Updates the asset name if only `extension` was supplied to assetCreate, and the default name is still in use.

Otherwise, will be ignored.

asset_filesize.description:
The full filesize for the upload in bytes. This field is required when an object in `parts` has `is_final` set to `true`.

Must be no more than 5 TiB.

post.description: Leave a Comment on an Asset

annotation.description:
Serialized list of geometry and/or drawing data.

Learn more [here](https://developer.frame.io/docs/workflows-assets/working-with-annotations)

page.description: Page number for a comment (documents only).

pitch.description: Pitch measurement for the comment (360deg video only).

timestamp.description:
Timestamp for the comment, in frames, starting at 0. 

Comments can "global" to an asset in which you don't provide the timestamp, or if you're reviewing a "document" like a PDF then you would use `page` instead of `timestamp` to identify where the comment belongs.

yaw.description: Yaw measurement for the comment (360deg video only).

duration.description: Used to produce range-based comments, this is the duration measured in frames.

private.description: Set to true to make your comment a "Team-only Comment" that won't be visible to anonymous reviewers or Collaborators.

get.description: This endpoint will return all comments and replies in a flat list.

Asset.description: Accepts arbitrary key/value pairs for storing information on an asset
Asset.title: Asset

LibrarySearchFilter.description: Custom ES filters to apply to your query
LibrarySearchFilter.title: LibrarySearchFilter

filter.description: Custom ES filters to apply to your query

include_deleted.description: Flag to include soft-deleted records in results

page_size.description: The number of results to include in the page

id.description: Identifier of an asset to be copied

V2BatchAssetsDestinationIdCopyPostRespon.description: Assets that were not copied, and why they weren't copied
V2BatchAssetsDestinationIdCopyPostRespon.title: V2BatchAssetsDestinationIdCopyPostResponsesContentApplicationJsonSchemaError

V2BatchAssetsDestinationIdCopyPostRespon.description: The assets that were succesfully copied
V2BatchAssetsDestinationIdCopyPostRespon.title: V2BatchAssetsDestinationIdCopyPostResponsesContentApplicationJsonSchemaSuccess

error.description: Assets that were not copied, and why they weren't copied

success.description: The assets that were succesfully copied

id.description: The ID for this asset, can be used in places like `https://app.frame.io/player/:id` to go directly to an asset.

name.description: Represents the name of the asset (filename).

number.description:
The part number/index, starting at 1. Part numbers may be skipped, and may be uploaded in any order, but will be used to concatinate the final file in sequentual order. 

Cannot be greater than 10,000 (the maximum number of parts that AWS allows).

size.description:
The size of the part in bytes. If the size does not abide by the [AWS Multi-Part upload restrictions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/qfacts.html), an error will be returned.

For instance, must be greater than or equal to 5 MiB, (5,242,880 bytes) is `is_final` is not t`true`.

is_final.description: Whether this is the final part of the file.

upload_urls.description:
Array of AWS S3 presigned upload PUT URLs.

Returned in the same order as the `parts` field of the request.

AuditEventEventDetails.description: The details for the event. This varies quite a bit from one type of event to another but there are some examples in the example responses.
AuditEventEventDetails.title: AuditEventEventDetails

AuditEventResourceType.description: The type of the referenced resource
AuditEventResourceType.title: AuditEventResourceType

AuditEventSource.description: The Source of the action that produced a given audit log entry
AuditEventSource.title: AuditEventSource

client.description:
The API client the audit event was triggered by. This will not always be populated.

When the API call that produced an event was made via an OAuth app it will look like: `oauth_app/:uuid`, and if it was made via a developer token it will look like: `user_token/:uuid`, with the UUID being the User ID the token belongs to

Here are some examples:

 - `user_token/57823879-e541-49b0-b902-c43794c4400c`
 - `oauth_app/80ccf0bb-ae91-49c4-8aec-8f6238c14947`
 - `web/2024-06-10-18-15`
 - `pipeline-media-warden/cbd0061f6f190489e8af3137b8707b33f498f53f`
 - `null`

event_details.description: The details for the event. This varies quite a bit from one type of event to another but there are some examples in the example responses.

ip_address.description: The IP address associated with this event

project_id.description: The Project ID that this action occured in

resource_id.description: The ID of the referenced resource

resource_type.description: The type of the referenced resource

source.description: The Source of the action that produced a given audit log entry

type.description: The type of the text element

deleted_at.description: If deleted, when this Project was deleted

email.description: Create an invite for the given email

message.description: Invite message to be used for this one bath op

team_id.description: Create a set of invites for all approved team members within the given team

user_group_id.description: Create a set of invites for all the users within the given user group

user_id.description: Create an invite for the given user_id

default_message.description: Default invite message to be applied to all invites created

send_email.description: When false no emails will be sent to the new Reviewers and Pending Reviewers

allow_approvals.description: Enable/disable changing an Asset's status by non-Collaborator/Team Members via the Review Link.

current_version_only.description: Show **only** the most recent version of Assets in the Review Link

deleted_at.description: Deletion time of the Review Link

enable_downloading.description: Enable or disable downloading on this Review Link. The default depends on whether the auth used to create this review link allows for this or not.

expires_at.description: Expiration time of the Review Link

is_active.description: Disable an Review Link by switching this from `true` -> `false`, and vice-versa.

password.description:
Actually a `passphrase`, this is an additional security measure for a second layer of security on your Review Links. 

 Supports alphanumeric input.

has_password.description: If false, the Review Link has no passphrase

item_count.description: Number of items included at the root of the Review Link
